LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS IS BASED UPON PERSONNEL MANAGEMENT ACTIVITY THAT IS INSUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT THE CLAIM EVEN IF IMPROPER MOTIVATION IS ALLEGED. MANAGING PERSONNEL IS NOT OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT BEYOND THE BONDS OF HUMAN DECENCY. (SEE JANKEN VS. G.M. HUGHES ELECTRONICS (1996) 46 CAL.APP. 4TH 55, 80, CITING COLE VS. FAIR OAKS FIRE PROTECTION DISTRICT (1987) 43 CAL.3D 148, 155.) NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECT COULD BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR 5797

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. The third cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress is based upon personnel management activity that is insufficient to support the claim even if improper motivation is alleged. Managing personnel is not outrageous conduct beyond the bonds of human decency. (See Janken vs. G.M. Hughes Electronics (1996) 46 Cal.App. 4th 55, 80, citing Cole vs. Fair Oaks Fire Protection District (1987) 43 Cal.3d 148, 155.) No reasonable probability that defect could be cured by amendment. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
